summary: Plan is architecturally sound and well-structured. The callback-based ConstellationOverlay design, spatial hash approach, and quality-tier gating are all correct. A few issues need fixing before implementation: per-frame findPairs is unnecessarily expensive, the type-unsafe cast in renderLoop should be avoided, and ParticleField's 2D particle model needs careful handling.

issues.title: Per-frame spatialHash.findPairs is expensive — pre-compute pairs at maxThreshold on rebuild instead
issues.detail: The plan calls `spatialHash.findPairs(effectiveThreshold, maxLines)` every frame with a bass-modulated threshold. For 2000 points (high tier) traversing 27 neighbor cells each, this is a significant per-frame CPU cost. Instead: during `rebuild()`, call `findPairs(maxThreshold, maxLines)` once and cache all pairs with their distances. In `draw()`, just filter the cached pairs by `dist <= effectiveThreshold` and write the survivors to the line buffer. This turns per-frame work from O(N * 27 cells) spatial hash traversal into a simple O(maxLines) linear filter.
issues.fix: In `rebuild()`: `this.cachedPairs = spatialHash.findPairs(config.maxThreshold, config.maxLines);`. In `draw()`: `const threshold = lerp(baseThreshold, maxThreshold, bassEnergy); const activePairs = this.cachedPairs.filter(p => p.dist <= threshold);` then write activePairs to the line buffer.

issues.title: Avoid `(d.geometrySystem as any).activeIndex` cast — pass activeIndex and transitioning via LoopDeps
issues.detail: The plan proposes casting `d.geometrySystem as any` to read `activeIndex` and `transitioning` in renderLoop.ts. This bypasses type safety and creates a fragile coupling. Since the plan already adds `constellationOverlay` to LoopDeps, also add two small callback fields.
issues.fix: Add `getActiveIndex?: (() => number) | null` and `getTransitioning?: (() => boolean) | null` to LoopDeps. In main.ts: `deps.getActiveIndex = () => modeManager.activeIndex; deps.getTransitioning = () => modeManager.transitioning;`. Use these in renderLoop instead of the any-cast.

issues.title: ParticleField uses 2D particles — positions getter must return the 3D BufferGeometry attribute, not reconstruct from Particle[]
issues.detail: ParticleField's `Particle` type has `{ x, y, vx, vy, ... }` — a 2D model. But the Three.js Points mesh has a 3D `position` BufferAttribute (z values exist in the GPU buffer even if they're 0 or small random offsets). The getter should return the actual Float32Array from the BufferGeometry attribute, not try to reconstruct from the 2D Particle array. This ensures constellation gets the same positions the GPU is rendering.
issues.fix: Instead of storing a separate `basePositions` reference, do: `get positions() { const attr = pointsMesh?.geometry?.getAttribute('position'); return attr ? new Float32Array((attr as THREE.BufferAttribute).array) : null; }`. This matches what the GPU sees.

issues.title: SpatialHash cellSize must equal maxThreshold for correctness across varying query thresholds
issues.detail: The SpatialHash constructor takes a `cellSize`. If pairs are pre-computed at `maxThreshold` per the fix above, `cellSize` should be set to `maxThreshold` (or slightly larger) at construction time. If cellSize < query threshold, pairs in non-adjacent cells would be missed. The plan doesn't specify how cellSize relates to threshold.
issues.fix: Document in SpatialHash that cellSize must be >= the largest threshold that will be queried. In `createConstellationOverlay`, construct SpatialHash with `cellSize = config.maxThreshold`.

issues.title: Systems array reference in main.ts — verify accessibility
issues.detail: The plan references `systems[modeManager.activeIndex]` in the getActivePositions callback in main.ts. But systems are passed to modeManager via factory objects, not stored in a top-level array. The plan needs to create this array explicitly.
issues.fix: Before the callback, declare: `const systems = [particles, ribbon, pointCloud, crystal];` in main.ts scope (matching the factory order in createModeManager). The plan's pseudocode already implies this but it should be explicit.

issues.title: uColor source not specified — needs palette integration
issues.detail: The plan mentions `uColor from palette` in the draw method but doesn't specify how palette color is obtained. The constellation overlay doesn't receive palette information through its config or callbacks.
issues.fix: Either: (a) add a `getBaseColor?: () => THREE.Color` callback to the factory params, or (b) use a fixed constellation color (e.g., `vec3(0.6, 0.7, 0.9)` — cool blue-white) as a hardcoded uniform, which is simpler and visually appropriate for constellation lines. Option (b) is recommended to keep the API minimal.

issues.title: Verify GLSL import convention matches existing pattern
issues.detail: Existing shaders are `.glsl` files likely imported via Vite's `?raw` suffix. The plan should note that the new shader files follow the same import convention.
issues.fix: In constellationOverlay.ts, import as: `import vertexShader from '../shaders/constellationLine.vert.glsl?raw';` matching the pattern used in pointCloud.ts, ribbonField.ts, etc.

confirmations: Quality tier extension (step 1) correctly places fields after enableSlowModulation — verified field exists in all 3 tiers
Fog convention in step 3 matches existing shaders: smoothstep(uFogNear, uFogFar, vDepth) with desaturation tint — verified across all 4 fragment shaders
GeometrySystem interface in types.ts has optional methods pattern (cleanup?, setOpacity?) — adding getMesh?() is consistent (step 5)
ModeManager exposes activeIndex and transitioning as readonly — verified in interface definition
pointCloud, ribbonField, and crystalField all have `get positions(): Float32Array | null` — verified, pattern is consistent
Callback-based architecture (step 4/7) correctly avoids expanding LoopDeps with system-specific knowledge
Hiding constellation during transitions (opacity 0) prevents visual discontinuity between point sets
constellation/ directory does not yet exist — all files_created paths are new
depthWrite: false + transparent: true + additive blending is correct for overlay lines composited over points
notes: The plan is well-designed overall. The callback pattern for getActivePositions/getActiveMesh keeps the constellation overlay decoupled from specific geometry systems. The main performance concern (per-frame spatial hash query) is straightforward to fix by caching pairs at rebuild time. After addressing the high-severity items, this plan is ready for implementation.
